successCriteria.description: The system recognizes the intent "none" as a deliberate no-op and produces no code, schema, or configuration changes.

constraints: No files may be created, modified, or deleted as a result of this intent.
No database migrations, schema changes, or seed data may be produced.
outOfScope: All feature work — no intent was provided, so there is nothing to scope in.

ambiguities.description: The intent is "none" — this could mean (a) no feature work is needed at this time, or (b) the intent was not provided / was lost in transit. The two interpretations lead to different outcomes: (a) means the cycle should be a no-op, (b) means the cycle should be escalated for human clarification.
options: No feature work needed — treat as intentional no-op
Intent was not provided — escalate for human clarification
